title: Service Registration Pattern — NXGN Actions API
purpose: **AI Summary**: How to register feature services (commands, queries, mappers) with DI. Each feature defines a static `Add{Feature}Services()` extension method in `Bootstrap/Features/`. A central `Services.cs` orchestrator chains all feature registrations. All services use `AddScoped`. Called from `Program.cs` via `builder.Services.AddServices()`.

examples.name: Folder Structure Example 1
examples.code:
Bootstrap/
├── Services.cs                              # Central orchestrator — chains all feature registrations
├── EntityFramework.cs                       # DbContext and factory registration
└── Features/
    ├── ActionsExtensions.cs                 # Add{Feature}Services() extension methods
    ├── WorkflowsExtensions.cs
    ├── ComplianceProfilesExtensions.cs
    ├── EscalationRulesExtensions.cs
    └── LanguagesExtensions.cs

examples.name: Per-Feature Extension Method Example 1
Per-Feature Extension Method Example 2
Per-Feature Extension Method Example 3
Central Orchestrator: Services.cs Example 1

sections.title: The Core Principle
content: > **Explicit DI registration, organized by feature.** No reflection scanning or auto-discovery for feature services (commands, queries, mappers). Each feature owns its DI registration. Note: EF configurations use `ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly()` for auto-discovery — the "no scanning" rule applies to DI registration, not EF model configuration (see [EF Configuration](ef-configuration.md)).

sections.title: Folder Structure
content: ├── Services.cs                              # Central orchestrator — chains all feature registrations
- One file per feature (matches `Features/{Entity}/` folder structure)
- Lives in `Bootstrap/Features/`, NOT inside the feature folder itself

sections.title: Per-Feature Extension Method
content: Each feature registers its services via a static extension method on `IServiceCollection`:
public static IServiceCollection AddWorkflowsServices(this IServiceCollection services)
Organize registrations by type within each method. Not all features need every section:
| **Commands** | SaveCommand, DeleteCommand, lifecycle commands | Every writable feature |
| **Queries** | GetQuery, GetGridListQuery, GetCountQuery, GetLookupQuery | Every readable feature |
| **Mappers** | EntityMapper, DTOMapper, DTOMapperDataContextService | Every feature with DTOs |
| **Services** | Business logic services (validation, workflow, translation) | Features with complex domain logic |
When a DTOMapper needs pre-loaded data from multiple sources, the `DTOMapperDataContextService` is a separate scoped registration:
public static IServiceCollection AddActionsServices(this IServiceCollection services)

When a parent feature has child features (e.g., Actions -> Evidence, Consultations), sub-feature commands/queries are registered in the parent's extension method since they live in the parent's `Shared/` folder:
public static IServiceCollection AddActionsServices(this IServiceCollection services)

sections.title: Central Orchestrator: Services.cs
content: `Services.cs` chains all feature registrations into a single call. Organized by domain area:
public static IServiceCollection AddServices(this IServiceCollection services)
- Comments group by domain area (not alphabetical)
- Every `Add{Feature}Services()` call has exactly one matching extension class
- New features add one line here and one new extension file

sections.title: Registration Rules
content: | **Always `AddScoped`** | Aligns with `DbContext` scoped lifetime. Forces stateless design. Never `AddSingleton` for feature services. |
| **Explicit registration** | No reflection scanning (`services.Scan(...)`) for feature services. Explicit is auditable and debuggable. |
| **No constructor injection in bootstrap** | Registration is purely declarative — `services.AddScoped<T>()`. Resolution happens at request time by FastEndpoints. |
| **Return `services`** | Every extension method returns `IServiceCollection` for fluent chaining. |
| **Primary constructors** | All registered services use primary constructors — `public class SaveWorkflowCommand(IDbContextFactory<ActionsDbContext> dbContextFactory, ...)`. No `_field` naming. |

sections.title: Departures from Capstone
content: | **Folder depth** | `Bootstrap/Features/{Area}/{Feature}Extensions.cs` | `Bootstrap/Features/{Feature}Extensions.cs` | Actions has fewer features; flat layout preferred |
| **Generic open types** | `services.AddScoped(typeof(SaveCommand<,>))` | Not used (no generic commands) | Actions features are concrete, not polymorphic |
| **DbContext registration** | `AddDbContextFactory` + `AddDbContextPool` (both) | `AddDbContextFactory` only | Actions uses factory pattern exclusively for scoped context creation |

| **Configuration style** | Static extension methods on `ModelBuilder` | `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>` with `ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly` | ADR-0018 — Actions chose per-entity configuration classes |

sections.title: See Also
content: - [Feature Implementation](feature-implementation.md) — Step-by-step guide referencing DI registration as the final step
